title: Post-and-screw album binding
materials: post-and-screw sets (aluminium, 3/4 inch and 1 inch lengths)
book-cloth or decorative paper for covers
sources: SYNTHESISED — post-and-screw binding is a modern commercial binding structure; no canonical pre-1928 PD source. Draws on general bookbinding geometry for cover construction.
notes: Post-and-screw (also called Chicago screw or sex-bolt) binding: the spine posts pass through drilled or punched holes in hard covers and loose pages, screwing together on the front cover face. Pages are fully removable. Good for photo albums, portfolios, sample books. Covers: cover construction, page-stock preparation, hole spacing and drilling, screw tightening without overtightening, adding extra pages with longer posts.
